expectations.text: Output shows all 3 stages: advocate evaluation, critic challenges, and arbiter decision
expectations.evidence: All 3 stages: Stage 1 Advocate Report, Stage 2 Critic Report, Stage 3 Arbiter Decision

expectations.text: Advocate evaluates strengths for both EKS and Lambda options
expectations.evidence: Advocate covers Option A (K8s/EKS) with 5 strengths (A-001 to A-005) and Option B (Lambda) with 5 strengths (A-006 to A-010)

expectations.text: Critic discusses Lambda cold start impact on the 30-second SLA
expectations.evidence: C-005: Cold start risk under SLA pressure (severity: high). Discusses 5-10s cold starts + 8s P95 = 13-18s vs 30s SLA
C-001: Operational complexity is real and ongoing (severity: medium). References node upgrades, K8s version upgrades, security patches

expectations.text: Arbiter ranks the options with clear reasoning
expectations.evidence: Rank 1: Kubernetes (EKS), Rank 2: Lambda. Clear decisive factors listed for each

expectations.text: Analysis specifically addresses the bursty 0-500 RPS traffic pattern
expectations.evidence: C-002 discusses HPA scaling lag for burst from 0 to 500 RPS. C-009 calculates 500 RPS * 8s = 4,000 concurrent Lambda executions
Final Recommendation: Option A — Kubernetes (EKS), Confidence: High (8/10)
